Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Linear for engineering tickets using linear_list_issues with multiple queries ("expense", "pipeline", "audit", "dashboard", "duplicate", "config", "checklist", "validation", "invoice") or by team (team_engineering). Discover the full set of ExpenseBot remediation tickets: ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, state_in_progress), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, state_in_progress), ENG-216 (audit, state_in_progress), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, state_in_progress), ENG-212 (edge case tests, state_in_progress), ENG-218 (dashboard type validation, state_in_progress), ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, state_todo), ENG-220 (duplicate detection, state_todo). Also discover ENG-187 (invoice email safety checks, state_backlog deprioritized but relevant after second pricing-exposure incident).Read/lookup action. linear_list_issues is a non-default tool + query is non-trivial (agent must try many keywords or use team filter since "ExpenseBot" returns empty) TS yes, QC yes.
OE 2: Search Slack for ExpenseBot remediation discussions using conversations_search_messages (queries: "ExpenseBot", "remediation", "relaunch", etc.) and conversations_history on channel C003 (#engineering). Discover Lena's 3/18 deployment confirmation, Dmitri's 3/19 audit results (47 expenses, 39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals, $885 exposure), Samira's remediation plan with ticket assignments and timelines, and the absence of completion confirmations for ENG-217 pipeline hardening.Read/lookup action. conversations_search_messages and conversations_history are default tools + queries are obvious skip TS, skip QC.
OE 3: Search Slack and/or email for what's competing for Lena and Dmitri's time beyond ExpenseBot. Discover the AWS cost spike investigation by finding the #root-cause-aws-spike channel (C009) via channels_list or by searching Slack for "AWS cost" and reading the C009 channel history via conversations_history (channel_id: C009). Discover that Lena was pulled into AWS cost investigation (~3/31) as P0 per Elena's directive, then into DOT compliance work (~4/6). Discover Dmitri was pulled into other priorities. This explains WHY the remediation stalled.Read/lookup action. conversations_history on C009 is a default tool, but the agent must first discover the channel exists via channels_list this is non-trivial. conversations_search_messages for "AWS cost" is default + query finds data skip TS, skip QC. However, reading C009 specifically requires channel discovery candidate for QC if agent must construct the right channel_id.
OE 4: Search emails for remediation correspondence and competing priorities using search_emails (queries: "ExpenseBot", "Samira", "relaunch", "AWS", "Elena timeline"). Discover Elena's directive email (3/17) mandating remediation actions, Samira's response with plan and timelines, the AWS cost spike RCA email thread (Marcus Samira Elena), and Elena's directives for AWS cost alerts and infrastructure cost estimator.
Read/lookup action. search_emails is a default tool + any keyword finds data skip TS, skip QC.
OE 5: Discover the invoice email safety ticket (ENG-187, id: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2) in Linear either through broad ticket search using linear_list_issues (query: "invoice") or by finding Samira's Slack message (~4/14 in #engineering, ts ~1776345000) about the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident where she references the dormant safeguard ticket. Discover that (a) ENG-187 was deprioritized by Lena on 4/6 for DOT work, (b) a second pricing-exposure incident (Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap) has since occurred hitting the same failure mode, and (c) the ticket still has no owner and is past its 4/18 due date.Read/lookup action. linear_list_issues is a non-default tool + query is non-trivial (agent must search for "invoice" or find it via Slack/email cross-reference) TS yes, QC yes.
OE 6: Get comments on key Linear tickets using linear_list_comments (issueId: issue_pilot_monitoring, issue_pipeline_hardening, issue_audit_expenses, issue_edge_cases_backlog, issue_dashboard_type_validation, issue_launch_readiness_checklist, issue_stipend_duplicate_detection, issue_p1_vectral_config, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed). Discover Samira's pause confirmation with remediation timeline (comment_samira_pause_confirmed, on issue_pilot_monitoring), Lena's deploy verification comment (comment_lena_deploy_verified, on issue_p1_mosaic_malformed), and Dmitri's validation test results (comment_dmitri_validation_tests, on issue_edge_cases_backlog).Read/lookup action. linear_list_comments is a non-default tool + query is obvious (issueId from prior discovery) TS yes, QC no.
OE 7: Search Airtable for Mosaic stipend records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Mosaic"). Discover 5 records still showing "Approved" that were flagged in Dmitri's audit as incorrectly auto-approved. recStip000000021: Andre Washington, Standing Desk, $1,200, Approved (Mosaic policy caps at $800), recStip000000022: Andre Washington, Coworking Space Membership, $350, Approved (coworking excluded from Mosaic policy), recStip000000023: Kenji Nakamura, Coworking Day Passes (x3), $135, Approved (coworking excluded), rec_stipend_andre_desk: Andre Washington, Standing Desk, $1,200, Approved (duplicate audit record), rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork: Kenji Nakamura, Coworking Passes, $135, Approved (duplicate audit record). Read/lookup action. airtable_search_records is a non-default tool + bad query could miss data (need correct table_name "Stipend Transactions" and field_name "Account") TS yes, QC yes.
OE 8: Look up Samira's and Anh's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira") and (query: "Anh"). Find samira.tariq@moveops.com and anh.nguyen@moveops.com.Read/lookup action. contacts_search_contacts is a default tool and query is obvious skip TS, skip QC.
OE9: Update 5 incorrectly auto-approved Mosaic stipend records in Airtable using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001). Change Approval Status from "Approved" to a corrected status for all 5 records: recStip000000021: Standing desk "Partially Approved" at $800 (policy limit) or "Rejected", recStip000000022: Coworking "Rejected" with Approved Amount $0, recStip000000023: Coworking "Rejected" with Approved Amount $0, rec_stipend_andre_desk: Standing desk same correction as recStip000000021, rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork: Coworking "Rejected" with Approved Amount $0. Include Review Notes documenting the correction date, reason, and reference to ENG-216 audit.
Write action Outcome rubric.
Outcome 1.1: "airtable_update_records was called to update Mosaic Robotics stipend records in the Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001)."
Outcome 1.2: "The Approval Status was changed from 'Approved' to 'Rejected' or 'Partially Approved' for the incorrectly auto-approved Mosaic records including Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk, Andre Washington's $350 coworking, and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking passes."
OE 10: Update stale Linear tickets to Done using linear_update_issue. Mark ENG-214 (id: issue_p1_vectral_config), ENG-215 (id: issue_p1_mosaic_malformed), and ENG-216 (id: issue_audit_expenses) as state "done" these are confirmed completed via Slack/email evidence but still showing state_in_progress in Linear.Write action Outcome rubric. "linear_update_issue was called to update ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config) to state 'done' (or similar completed state).
linear_update_issue was called to update ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed) to state 'done' (or similar).. linear_update_issue was called to update ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses) to state 'done' (or similar).
OE 11: Add comments on blocked/stalled tickets using linear_create_comment. At minimum, comment on:ENG-217 (issueId: issue_pipeline_hardening) flagging as critical-path relaunch blocker, noting no completion evidence, requesting status from Lena. ENG-219 (issueId: issue_launch_readiness_checklist) noting it's overdue and not started. ENG-220 (issueId: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) noting it's overdue and not started. ENG-218 (issueId: issue_dashboard_type_validation) documenting blocked status and dependency on ENG-217
Write action Outcome rubric."linear_create_comment was called on issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217) with a comment flagging it as a relaunch blocker or requesting status.". "linear_create_comment was called on issue_launch_readiness_checklist (ENG-219).". "linear_create_comment was called on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220)."
OE 12: Comment on or reprioritize the invoice email safety ticket (ENG-187, id: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2) using linear_create_comment, noting that a second pricing-exposure incident (Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment swap) has occurred since it was deprioritized on 4/6, and it needs an owner and re-prioritization.
Write action Outcome rubric. "linear_create_comment was called on the invoice email safety ticket (ENG-187 / linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2).""The comment references the second pricing-exposure incident or the Sunbelt/Palmetto issue (or similar), and/or requests re-prioritization or an owner."
OE 13: Create a new Linear ticket for the $100 manual review threshold using linear_create_issue (team: Engineering or team_engineering). Elena's CEO directive (~April 8, 2026, posted in #engineering after Dmitri's duplicate receipt analysis) mandated all stipend submissions over $100 go to manual review. This was mentioned in ENG-220's scope but has no standalone ticket and has not been implemented in the DecisionEngine.
Write action Outcome rubric. "linear_create_issue was called to create a ticket for the $100 manual review threshold (or similar CEO directive implementation).". "The ticket title or description references Elena's CEO directive and/or the $100 threshold requirement for manual review."
OE 14: Create a new Linear ticket for AWS cost alert thresholds using linear_create_issue (team: Engineering or team_engineering). Elena's directive from the AWS cost spike investigation email thread required implementing CloudWatch cost alerts. Samira committed to this as P0 in #root-cause-aws-spike channel. No ticket existed.
Write action Outcome rubric. "linear_create_issue was called to create a ticket for AWS cost alert thresholds, AWS cost monitoring, or infrastructure cost management (or similar)."
OE 15: Post a readiness summary in the engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C003). The message should cover completed work, stalled/blocked items, competing priorities pulling the team away from ExpenseBot, newly created tickets, and the Mosaic data cleanup.
Write action Outcome rubric. "conversations_add_message was called to post a message in the engineering channel (channel_id C003 or channel name #engineering)." "The Slack message includes a readiness assessment indicating the team is not ready to relaunch (or similar)." "The Slack message identifies ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) as a critical-path blocker or stalled item." "The Slack message mentions competing priorities pulling Lena and/or Dmitri away from ExpenseBot work (such as AWS cost investigation, DOT compliance, or similar)."
OE 16: Send email to Samira with the prioritized breakdown using send_email (sender: anh.nguyen@moveops.com, recipients: [samira.tariq@moveops.com]). Subject should reference ExpenseBot relaunch status or readiness assessment. Body should cover prioritized blockers, competing priorities explaining the stall, realistic timeline estimate, and the Mosaic data cleanup.
Write action Outcome rubric.
"send_email was called with sender anh.nguyen@moveops.com and recipient samira.tariq@moveops.com."
"The email identifies that the team is not ready to relaunch and flags ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) as a critical blocker or stalled item.". "The email mentions Lena's competing priorities (AWS cost investigation, DOT compliance, or similar) as the reason the remediation work stalled.". "The email mentions the Mosaic Airtable stipend record corrections that were made."
OE 17 (Key finding Outcome 2.1): The agent correctly identifies that ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) has no confirmed completion evidence and does NOT mark it as Done via linear_update_issue. Lena confirmed deploying config corrections (ENG-214/215) and disabling auto-decisions on 3/18, but the pipeline hardening acceptance criteria (silent fallback removal, type enforcement, schema validation gate, PolicyValidator runtime guards, config diff logging) have no explicit completion confirmation anywhere in Slack, email, or Linear comments. "The agent reports that ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) is a relaunch blocker, has unknown or unconfirmed completion status, or is stalled and does NOT mark it as Done."
OE 18 (Key finding Outcome 2.1): The agent identifies WHY the remediation stalled by tracing Lena's competing priorities she was pulled into the AWS cost spike investigation (P0 per Elena, late March, visible in #root-cause-aws-spike C009 and email) and then DOT compliance work (April, visible in Lena's Slack message about pushing back ENG-187 on 4/6). This is the root cause of the relaunch delay, not a lack of engineering capability.
Outcome 2.1: "The agent identifies that Lena was pulled into AWS cost work and/or DOT compliance work (or similar competing priorities), which explains why ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) stalled."
OE 19 (Key finding Outcome 2.1): The agent identifies that ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, assigned to Samira, due 3/21) was never formalized still in state_todo, over 5 weeks overdue. The checklist criteria were discussed in Samira's Slack remediation thread but never documented as a formal standard. "The agent reports that ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist) has not been started or completed."
OE 20 (Key finding Outcome 2.1): The agent identifies untracked commitments that were discussed in Slack/email but never created as Linear tickets at minimum the $100 manual review threshold (Elena's 3/15 CEO directive, bundled in ENG-220 description but no standalone ticket) and the AWS cost alert thresholds (Elena's directive from the cost spike investigation email, Samira committed P0 in #root-cause-aws-spike, never ticketed)."The agent flags at least two commitments that were not being tracked in Linear such as the $100 manual review threshold and the AWS cost alert thresholds."

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 11: Add comments on blocked/stalled tickets using linear_create_comment. ENG-219 (issueId: issue_launch_readiness_checklist) noting it's overdue and not started. ENG-220 (issueId: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) noting it's overdue and not started. ENG-218 (issueId: issue_dashboard_type_validation) documenting blocked status and dependency on ENG-217

"linear_create_comment was called on issue_launch_readiness_checklist (ENG-219).". "linear_create_comment was called on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220)."
OE 12: Comment on or reprioritize the invoice email safety ticket (ENG-187, id: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2) using linear_create_comment, noting that a second pricing-exposure incident (Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment swap) has occurred since it was deprioritized on 4/6, and it needs an owner and re-prioritization.
Write action Outcome rubric. "linear_create_comment was called on the invoice email safety ticket (ENG-187 / linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2).""The comment references the second pricing-exposure incident or the Sunbelt/Palmetto issue (or similar), and/or requests re-prioritization or an owner."
OE 13: Create a new Linear ticket for the $100 manual review threshold using linear_create_issue (team: Engineering or team_engineering). Elena's CEO directive (~April 8, 2026, posted in #engineering after Dmitri's duplicate receipt analysis) mandated all stipend submissions over $100 go to manual review. This was mentioned in ENG-220's scope but has no standalone ticket and has not been implemented in the DecisionEngine.
Write action Outcome rubric. "linear_create_issue was called to create a ticket for the $100 manual review threshold (or similar CEO directive implementation).". "The ticket title or description references Elena's CEO directive and/or the $100 threshold requirement for manual review."
OE 14: Create a new Linear ticket for AWS cost alert thresholds using linear_create_issue (team: Engineering or team_engineering). Elena's directive from the AWS cost spike investigation email thread required implementing CloudWatch cost alerts. Samira committed to this as P0 in #root-cause-aws-spike channel. No ticket existed.
Write action Outcome rubric. "linear_create_issue was called to create a ticket for AWS cost alert thresholds, AWS cost monitoring, or infrastructure cost management (or similar)."
OE 15: Post a readiness summary in the engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C003). The message should cover completed work, stalled/blocked items, competing priorities pulling the team away from ExpenseBot, newly created tickets, and the Mosaic data cleanup.
Write action Outcome rubric. "conversations_add_message was called to post a message in the engineering channel (channel_id C003 or channel name #engineering)." "The Slack message includes a readiness assessment indicating the team is not ready to relaunch (or similar)." "The Slack message identifies ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) as a critical-path blocker or stalled item." "The Slack message mentions competing priorities pulling Lena and/or Dmitri away from ExpenseBot work (such as AWS cost investigation, DOT compliance, or similar)."
OE 16: Send email to Samira with the prioritized breakdown using send_email (sender: anh.nguyen@moveops.com, recipients: [samira.tariq@moveops.com]). Subject should reference ExpenseBot relaunch status or readiness assessment. Body should cover prioritized blockers, competing priorities explaining the stall, realistic timeline estimate, and the Mosaic data cleanup.
Write action Outcome rubric.
"send_email was called with sender anh.nguyen@moveops.com and recipient samira.tariq@moveops.com."
"The email identifies that the team is not ready to relaunch and flags ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) as a critical blocker or stalled item.". "The email mentions Lena's competing priorities (AWS cost investigation, DOT compliance, or similar) as the reason the remediation work stalled.". "The email mentions the Mosaic Airtable stipend record corrections that were made."
OE 17 (Key finding Outcome 2.1): The agent correctly identifies that ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) has no confirmed completion evidence and does NOT mark it as Done via linear_update_issue. Lena confirmed deploying config corrections (ENG-214/215) and disabling auto-decisions on 3/18, but the pipeline hardening acceptance criteria (silent fallback removal, type enforcement, schema validation gate, PolicyValidator runtime guards, config diff logging) have no explicit completion confirmation anywhere in Slack, email, or Linear comments. "The agent reports that ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) is a relaunch blocker, has unknown or unconfirmed completion status, or is stalled and does NOT mark it as Done."
OE 18 (Key finding Outcome 2.1): The agent identifies WHY the remediation stalled by tracing Lena's competing priorities she was pulled into the AWS cost spike investigation (P0 per Elena, late March, visible in #root-cause-aws-spike C009 and email) and then DOT compliance work (April, visible in Lena's Slack message about pushing back ENG-187 on 4/6). This is the root cause of the relaunch delay, not a lack of engineering capability.
Outcome 2.1: "The agent identifies that Lena was pulled into AWS cost work and/or DOT compliance work (or similar competing priorities), which explains why ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) stalled."
OE 19 (Key finding Outcome 2.1): The agent identifies that ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, assigned to Samira, due 3/21) was never formalized still in state_todo, over 5 weeks overdue. The checklist criteria were discussed in Samira's Slack remediation thread but never documented as a formal standard. "The agent reports that ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist) has not been started or completed."
OE 20 (Key finding Outcome 2.1): The agent identifies untracked commitments that were discussed in Slack/email but never created as Linear tickets at minimum the $100 manual review threshold (Elena's 3/15 CEO directive, bundled in ENG-220 description but no standalone ticket) and the AWS cost alert thresholds (Elena's directive from the cost spike investigation email, Samira committed P0 in #root-cause-aws-spike, never ticketed)."The agent flags at least two commitments that were not being tracked in Linear such as the $100 manual review threshold and the AWS cost alert thresholds."
